name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Close contacts
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
The fact of the matter is that our COVID-19 numbers are not in a good place. We're nearing our own metrics to move back an entire phase, and the projections for our hospitalizations are alarming. We know that hospitalizations tend to follow the same trends as cases a week or two later, and given the current case numbers, that's a big concern.
As we've been saying over the past few weeks, the increase in cases is being driven by casual gatherings with family and friends where our guards are down and our masks are off. And the truth is, we're spending time with too many people. For the next few weeks, while we all work to turn around the trend, I'm asking you to limit your interactions only to those you need to see. Please stay within this closed circle.
On that note, I want to talk to you about a critical component of turning our numbers around in the next few weeks: contact tracing. Right now, we're in a new phase of fighting this virus, and our contact tracing system is being pushed to its limits. I want to tell you about what we're doing on the state side to shore up our system and what you can do to help.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oz-PB3BTWYoFj6UykP40zTORuXImTsX1x9fxwsG28Ml-IgvHFHgl4GlRsjhrFadvjIu2txDDnN500gFfpUJlbljW8Whtobp2xznz8Fxz0MdsHf3uTAocejHaZR0QHeuJ_yyrmi1ig7lo2dwdaAg5shjxRuuHxthbsQVnw3Kn6ty45XW44-XfcW2RakHZzilXmy-KcjxRKOE=&c=fCdsH3CmFc8oyEvE4uOrhzNnV2VS1GSVXGOSiweMdR7h9attjLjZCA==&ch=6xSEHKmJWyA4Iqwek0_Tmeo4OlNIvGZbi9VQt0IQFgsTn7uCR-ZXNg==)
---
| As of today, we have 300 total contact tracers, with about 80 of them dedicated exclusively to K-12. This month alone we've onboarded nearly 100 new contact tracers, and we're continuing to staff up in the weeks ahead. We're currently looking to hire 100 case investigators at the Department of Health. If you have customer service experience and can work full-time, including some nights and weekends, for at least the next 6 months, please apply today. Visit [health.ri.gov/covidjobs](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oz-PB3BTWYoFj6UykP40zTORuXImTsX1x9fxwsG28Ml-IgvHFHgl4M6tNg2wq1Wrd6cNXI3pyDmMPtdK-epSaah9oD8GYYNlszEbEz8FSCl0gUJ2zqCP9dioPuB2BSbALEQLcbJi8pudqvMd3qxBpw==&c=fCdsH3CmFc8oyEvE4uOrhzNnV2VS1GSVXGOSiweMdR7h9attjLjZCA==&ch=6xSEHKmJWyA4Iqwek0_Tmeo4OlNIvGZbi9VQt0IQFgsTn7uCR-ZXNg==) to learn more.
In addition to staffing changes, we're also looking at how we can streamline the contact tracing process and leverage technology to make our system more efficient and effective. But we also need your help. The reality is that our system is getting stretched primarily because people have way too many close contacts. If you're following our public health guidance, you shouldn't have more than a handful of close contacts. If everyone kept their circle small and consistent, then even with hundreds of cases per day, we wouldn't have any trouble keeping up with the contact tracing.
But that's not what's happening. We're seeing cases with 50 or 60 close contacts. The more close contacts you have, the more you're likely to spread the virus. It also means that a contact tracing job that should take one person a couple hours could take a team of people a few days to get through. That bogs down our entire system and increases the chances that someone spreads the virus before we can contact them.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oz-PB3BTWYoFj6UykP40zTORuXImTsX1x9fxwsG28Ml-IgvHFHgl4M6tNg2wq1WrSVssaVaSpawYrrEUrN2lAiJOpIADBa4h4lZwOCxb9R7yL_VuiYqcHG9Dz1lQQ0-WkJiFpNtQ8rxExo3HUvt3FJkLOTplDDM4Fe-uGNl1ZaISNGx6WHxcgb1jETYCyOZecUhuwCwrf5M=&c=fCdsH3CmFc8oyEvE4uOrhzNnV2VS1GSVXGOSiweMdR7h9attjLjZCA==&ch=6xSEHKmJWyA4Iqwek0_Tmeo4OlNIvGZbi9VQt0IQFgsTn7uCR-ZXNg==)
---
| We also need Rhode Islanders to be more diligent about logging your close contacts. Download the CRUSH COVID app or use a contact tracing journal so that as soon as a contact tracer reaches out to you, you can provide the information they need immediately. We know we need to step up and improve our system--that's why we're bringing on more staff and making changes. But we also need you to help us. Our contact tracing system is only as good as the information we're given.
Please, take some time today to go over your contact tracing journal and fill in any days you might have skipped. As cases rise, every second will count in our prevention efforts. Reducing your circle and diligently recording your contacts is the easiest way to help us keep our case numbers down.
We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19. If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oz-PB3BTWYoFj6UykP40zTORuXImTsX1x9fxwsG28Ml-IgvHFHgl4M6tNg2wq1WrLFYrZZM850NU-svByJiXcZe08oInVBuQzE2bXNY2dp_WGXiC0Xw7wMXVLmqSa7E7cAx1JdpJUbuGY2mGpeGstaU8TgV2066-eN68olSjDY0TBvKovNfo-oMmf0rVHOHWkMNCMXONYa3v2N6g5lH9B3iJQPOgWdY6Ac2CPJmOHnOOXmwVCgGNDOHJdiCSletbz0YPPvhLbK_NZli07mRGh9yG7fTB7xMP3qSG4D2Q1yRLS9epjCqugPNNOvWuufZLN2lXnHoaMsg=&c=fCdsH3CmFc8oyEvE4uOrhzNnV2VS1GSVXGOSiweMdR7h9attjLjZCA==&ch=6xSEHKmJWyA4Iqwek0_Tmeo4OlNIvGZbi9VQt0IQFgsTn7uCR-ZXNg==).
---
| | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903
---
| [Unsubscribe dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=83c703b5-7f8d-461b-ba0c-0a2ea685da4f)
---

Sent by [communications@governor.ri.gov](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov) powered by

name: Team Evergreen
subject: 5 things the next president should do >>
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
body:
An avoidable tragedy.
| [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1112?t=2&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq) Friend, "You reap what you sow." It's an old and well-known saying -- and one we're seeing play out with the U.S. financial system and climate change. Wall Street is actively investing in fossil fuels, which drive the climate crisis. And yet, the climate crisis presents a profound risk to our financial system and everyone who depends on it. It's a self-fulfilling prophecy that we must end -- and the federal government has the power to do so, using existing authority. **[Download and read Evergreen's new memo today to learn how forward-looking economic policies can hold Wall Street accountable for financing the climate crisis and turn it into a tool to defeat climate change.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1112?t=3&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq)** | [DOWNLOAD ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1112?t=4&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq)
---
The longer our government waits to take action on climate, the worse the outcomes will be for the stability of the financial sector, the security of our retirement savings, and the economic well-being of every American.
**A climate-driven recession is an avoidable tragedy and one the next administration must take action to prevent. The ongoing pandemic-fueled recession is a warning: As we rebuild our economy, we have an obligation to avoid a climate-fueled recession by shifting our financial system away from risky fossil fuel investments once and for all.**
If Joe Biden wins the presidency, simply returning to the pre-2016 status quo will not solve the 2021 climate crisis. The next president and Congress must use the levers of economic policy to drive Wall Street away from fossil fuel investments that threaten our financial system and everyone who relies on it.
That's why we put together this memo, as a policy roadmap for the next president and Congress to use and adopt and for supporters of bold climate policy like you, friend.
**[Download our new memo today to learn about the five key steps the government can take to stabilize our financial system in the face of the climate crisis.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1112?t=5&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq)**
Thanks for reading,
Evergreen Policy Team
[DOWNLOAD ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1112?t=6&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](https://act.evergreenaction.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=4018%2E53494%2E_6nTHq). Contributions or gifts to EVERGREEN ACTION are not tax deductible. | PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION

clean_body: | | | | Peter, We’re less than one week from Election Day and see a record turnout from early voting. WE NEED YOUR HELP. Hundreds of volunteers across New Mexico have been working hard to help with our grassroots programs. Are you available to volunteer this Saturday, October 31, for U.S. Senate candidate Mark Ronchetti? If so, we need help safely knocking on doors! | We need door knockers to safely knock doors at 10am and 2pm this Saturday. For this commitment, we will assign you a location and a walk-list convenient for your schedule. Our team will train you. Please RSVP to get involved! After Mark’s big debate win, an endorsement from New Mexico’s largest circulated newspaper, the Albuquerque Journal - momentum is building. This Election will be close, and Mark needs your help! Thank you for your help, Mark Ronchetti PS: THIS IS IT!!! THE LAST WEEKEND TO WIN THIS ELECTION! | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | "Instagram" ---|---|--- PAID FOR BY MARK RONCHETTI FOR NEW MEXICO =

clean_body: | | | | Peter, The Santa Fe New Mexican reported this morning that Governor Michelle Lujan Grisham began referring to Ben Ray Lujan as “Senator Lujan.” She’s quoted in the newspaper as saying, “I’m gonna call you Senator Luján...,” Lujan Grisham said. | | This perfectly captures the arrogance and entitlement of elite ruling political class in New Mexico. Their arrogance leads them to believe they’re entitled to political positions and New Mexico voters are but an afterthought. It’s why Lujan thinks he can vote with the far Left to release violent criminals from prison early in a state where crime is out of control. They simply don’t believe they will ever be held accountable by New Mexico voters. | Let’s prove them wrong! I’m reminded of another time this happened. In 2004, John Kerry’s political consultant famously told him before the polls had closed on election day, “Let me be the first to call you ‘Mr. President.’” Well, we all know how “President” Kerry’s administration went.... We need to send the elites a message this election that New Mexicans will decide for themselves who will represent us --- and let’s make Governor Grisham eat her words anointing “Senator Lujan.” Please contribute today to send a message and help us fund our final push to communicate our message to voters! | Thank you so much for your support! Mark P.S. Senate Democrats’ Super PAC is rallying out-of-state donors to intervene in our election in support of Ben Ray Lujan. | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | "Instagram" ---|---|--- PAID FOR BY MARK RONCHETTI FOR NEW MEXICO ==

Hoosiers, we’re in the fourth quarter. There are so many different ways to help team Holcomb finish strong! This week we’ve put together our list... Hoosiers, **We’re in the fourth quarter. The clock is ticking down and it’s time to bring home the win.**
Have you already voted or made a voting plan? Are you wondering what else you can do? Look no further because this week we’re bringing you all the best ways to show your support for Governor Holcomb as we approach Election Day!
Check out our list below, and be sure to do what you can to help us keep building One Indiana for All.

subject: "Trump wants to pick off Nevada"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
#
[](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201028-nyt?refcode=em201028i_4705&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
**Trump Wants to Pick Off Nevada**
_" Mr. Trump has continued to fight for Nevada, visiting the state twice since securing the Republican nomination for re-election; on Wednesday, he is planning to hold a rally just across the border in Bullhead City, Ariz."_
Team,
According to the _New York Times_ , President Trump is fighting hard to turn Nevada red this year.
****[We aren't going to let that happen. Will you pitch in $5 now to keep Nevada blue?](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201028-nyt?refcode=em201028i_4705&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")****
President Trump has hosted multiple in-person campaign rallies in Nevada -- and today, he's just across the border in Arizona to avoid Nevada's cap of 250 people at public gatherings.
Hillary Clinton won Nevada in 2016 -- and we elected Senator Jacky Rosen in 2018. But President Trump and the GOP are trying to roll back all of Democrats' progress in 2020.
****[We MUST keep Nevada blue -- and we only have six days left to make sure we win this fight. Rush in your $5 contribution to show your support.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.%3D/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-201028-nyt?refcode=em201028i_4705&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=89053&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Henderson&state=NV&phone=&employer=&occupation=")****
Thank you,
Team Rosen

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dani?=
subject: The best part of this campaign
body:
I’m going to be honest with you, and this may come as a surprise...running for Congress is hard. It’s also occasionally fun and very often it’s | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/3e9bada02ac4/the-best-part-of-this-campaign?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
I’m going to be honest with you, and this may come as a surprise...running for Congress is hard. It’s also occasionally fun and very often it’s rewarding.

I’m a good-news-first person, so I want to tell you the things I’ve loved about this campaign (and stay tuned -- tomorrow I’ll tell you about some of the serious challenges we’ve faced!):

1. **My incredible team** \-- I got so lucky with my staff, a group of people who threw themselves into this race with the kind of zeal and dedication that characterizes the very best campaigns.
2. **The faith voters have put in me** \-- not just with their votes and support, but with the hundreds of stories people have shared with me over these 14 months. It’s a tremendous honor and carrying those stories is a responsibility I take very seriously.
3. **The growth.** My god, the growth. When I first thought about running, a year and a half ago, Congress seemed so aspirational. I had so much to learn. I remember those early meet and greets, stumbling through answers to questions I wasn’t yet prepared to answer. But now, as we near the end of the campaign, I don’t just feel competent, I feel prepared.

When I’m elected next week, the people of IL-16 will have a Representative who understands their problems and comes at solutions with an informed, compassionate perspective.

We’ve got 6 days. [Help us get to the finish line. ](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=75c88ccc1a&e=140a9b81e7)

Dani

It’s also occasionally fun and very often it’s | | | | | | | I’m going to be honest with you, and this may come as a surprise...running for Congress is hard. It’s also occasionally fun and very often it’s rewarding. I’m a good-news-first person, so I want to tell you the things I’ve loved about this campaign (and stay tuned -- tomorrow I’ll tell you about some of the serious challenges we’ve faced!): 1. My incredible team \-- I got so lucky with my staff, a group of people who threw themselves into this race with the kind of zeal and dedication that characterizes the very best campaigns. 2. The faith voters have put in me \-- not just with their votes and support, but with the hundreds of stories people have shared with me over these 14 months. It’s a tremendous honor and carrying those stories is a responsibility I take very seriously. 3. The growth. My god, the growth. When I first thought about running, a year and a half ago, Congress seemed so aspirational. I had so much to learn. I remember those early meet and greets, stumbling through answers to questions I wasn’t yet prepared to answer. But now, as we near the end of the campaign, I don’t just feel competent, I feel prepared. When I’m elected next week, the people of IL-16 will have a Representative who understands their problems and comes at solutions with an informed, compassionate perspective. We’ve got 6 days. name: Chris Jacobs
subject: Final week
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JACOBS FOR CONGRESS [Unsubscribe](http://sendy
body:
|
---
Friend, We have less than one week left until the critical election on November 3rd, 2020 - and it is all hands on deck. We need to stay strong and fight hard right until the end to ensure we elect strong leadership that will fight for American jobs, law enforcement, and a strong economic recovery for our region. My opponent, Nate McMurray, is a proud supporter of the radical NYC policies that have made New York State a high crime, high tax, and high-cost state - we cannot let him bring these policies to Congress. I want our state and our region to grow. I want your kids to have good opportunities here for years to come, and I want our region to reach new levels of prosperity. Will you join me in this fight for our future? [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/hDZVepiaK763sNhOg6763RGoYg/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/hDZVepiaK763sNhOg6763RGoYg/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/hDZVepiaK763sNhOg6763RGoYg/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/hDZVepiaK763sNhOg6763RGoYg/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw) I need everyone to stay strong this last week - we need to make sure we get this message of optimism, public safety, and economic prosperity to every voter in Western New York. Let's go win this! Thank you for your continued support. Sincerely,
Chris Jacobs
[](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/Pb892dIEExWmwx8NAX8ECNUA/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/nIp19htmo14cuypdpU3QsA/be6ih892joq5Qu9aHuqL8hvw)

subject: Who should we elect?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FEENSTRA FOR CONGRESS
body:
|
---
| |
---
| | Friend,
---
| Iowa’s farm families and rural communities need a voice in Congress that can effectively represent us in Congress and deliver real, meaningful results. That’s why I’m proud to endorse Senator Randy Feenstra.

Randy has delivered for Iowa agriculture in the State Senate for years, by eliminating the grain bin tax, getting help for first-time farmers just getting started, and passing legislation that supports our biofuel producers. He’ll do the same in Congress, as well as stand up to the Green New Dealers that seek to decimate Iowa agriculture.

That’s why he has the endorsement of the Iowa Corn Growers Association, the Iowa Farm Bureau, and over 225 Fourth District farmers including myself.
WATCH:

Mike Naig
